reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 3 (Code Verification) triggered: incorrect code reading. Phase 03 claims 'ValidateSuggestedBlock is never invoked before execution in the main processing pipeline.' However, ValidateSuggestedBlock IS called in the P2P block reception path - both SyncServer.cs:197 and BlockDownloader.cs:214 call _blockValidator.ValidateSuggestedBlock() BEFORE blocks are suggested to BlockTree (which then triggers BlockchainProcessor). ValidateSuggestedBlock (BlockValidator.cs:70-136) calls ValidateTransactions (line 82), which invokes _txValidator.IsWellFormed (line 281). TxValidator (TxValidator.cs:20-78) includes GasLimitCapTxValidator.Instance for ALL transaction types (Legacy line 28, AccessList line 40, EIP1559 line 51, Blob line 64, SetCode line 76). GasLimitCapTxValidator.IsWellFormed (line 385-390) checks transaction.GasLimit > gasLimitCap and rejects violations. The 01e invariant 'Block-level gas cap re-check must pass before any transaction in the block executes' (file: outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W1B2_1771748568.json, lines 372-387) IS enforced - gas cap validation occurs in the synchronization layer before block execution. Phase 03's attack scenario 'Attacker constructs block with transactions exceeding EIP-7825 gas cap and propagates via P2P' fails because such blocks are rejected at SyncServer/BlockDownloader validation before reaching execution.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Block-level gas cap re-check must pass before any transaction in the block executes. Assertion: forall tx in B: block_gas_cap_check(tx) before execute(tx)
